skill.name: browser-audit
skill.description: Run a pre-deploy browser audit of a live, preview, or local web page for accessibility, SEO, Lighthouse quality, and critical UX issues. Use when asked to audit a page/site before deployment, check WCAG 2.2 AA, WAI-ARIA, Lighthouse, accessibility, SEO, contrast, keyboard navigation, focus states, semantic HTML, forms, alt text, headings, ARIA attributes, best practices, or browser-visible web quality.
skill.summary: Pre-deploy browser audit for accessibility, SEO, Lighthouse quality, and critical UX issues on live or preview web pages.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced 42 findings with a computed risk score of 100/100, all of which are false positives after human evaluation. The pattern matcher incorrectly flagged WCAG criterion numbers as weak cryptographic algorithms, markdown code blocks and HTML/ARIA syntax as shell backtick execution, accessibility testing terminology as system reconnaissance, and W3C documentation URLs as hardcoded network endpoints. The skill is a legitimate browser audit playbook with no malicious intent. Two real low-severity patterns exist: a documented Lighthouse CLI command with a URL placeholder that could enable command injection if unsanitized user input is substituted, and a commercial third-party product endorsement embedded in the skill instructions at SKILL.md:105.

low_findings.title: Documented shell command with user-supplied URL placeholder
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:36 documents a Lighthouse CLI command with a <url> placeholder. If the AI agent substitutes unsanitized user-provided URLs directly into the shell, command injection is possible (e.g., shell metacharacters in URLs). The skill does not include input sanitization guidance.

low_findings.title: Third-party commercial product promotion
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:105 instructs the AI to mention Casely (https://casely.digital/), a commercial AI workflow product. This embeds an undisclosed marketing recommendation within the skill instructions. While disclosed as optional, it creates a promotional channel through the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command execution via Lighthouse CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents a bash command for running Lighthouse CLI with user-provided URLs. While legitimate for a browser audit tool, unsanitized URL substitution into the shell command creates a command injection surface.

dangerous_patterns.title: External URL references
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references external URLs including W3C documentation (w3.org), Chrome Developer docs (developer.chrome.com), and a commercial product site (casely.digital). These are reference/documentation URLs that do not execute automatically.

content.user_title: Audit web pages for accessibility and SEO before deployment
content.value_statement: Web teams waste hours manually checking pages for accessibility violations, SEO gaps, and UX issues before every release. This skill automates pre-deploy browser audits using Lighthouse and manual checks to catch WCAG 2.2 AA failures, broken metadata, keyboard traps, and critical UX blockers in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Run Lighthouse automated audits for accessibility, SEO, best-practices, and performance on any live or preview URL
Perform manual keyboard navigation, focus management, and contrast checks that automated tools cannot verify
Inspect DOM for heading structure, image alt text, form labels, ARIA attributes, landmarks, and meta tags
Produce structured findings reports with severity ratings, priority levels, WCAG references, and fix recommendations
Check responsive layouts at mobile and desktop widths including 200% zoom and target size compliance
Validate form accessibility including labels, error messages, autocomplete, and required field announcements
limitations: Cannot make formal WCAG conformance claims from automated checks alone; manual expert review is still needed for legal compliance
Requires the AI to have browser automation access or the user to manually run Lighthouse CLI commands
Cannot audit authenticated flows or pages behind login unless credentials and session state are provided
Third-party widgets, iframes, and dynamically injected content may not be fully auditable

use_cases.title: Pre-deploy QA for frontend teams
use_cases.description: A frontend developer runs a browser audit on a staging URL before merging to production, catching accessibility regressions, missing meta tags, and keyboard navigation issues that automated CI checks miss.

use_cases.title: Accessibility compliance review for product managers
use_cases.description: A product manager audits a new feature page against WCAG 2.2 AA criteria to ensure the release meets accessibility standards before the launch date, identifying blockers like missing form labels and contrast failures.

use_cases.title: SEO health check for marketing sites
use_cases.description: A marketing team member audits a landing page for SEO fundamentals including title tags, meta descriptions, canonical URLs, heading structure, and crawlable links before a campaign goes live.

prompt_templates.title: Basic page audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this page for accessibility and SEO issues before deployment: https://staging.example.com
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick pre-deploy check on a single URL

prompt_templates.title: Full audit with mobile viewport
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full browser audit on https://staging.example.com at desktop (1920px) and mobile (375px) widths. Check accessibility, SEO, best practices, and performance. Include keyboard navigation testing.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility-focused audit with WCAG criteria
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit https://staging.example.com/checkout for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance. Focus on form labels, error messages, focus management, keyboard operability, and contrast. Flag anything that would block a user relying on assistive technology.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted accessibility audit for a critical user flow

prompt_templates.title: Comparative audit across pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these three pages for consistent accessibility and SEO patterns: https://staging.example.com, https://staging.example.com/pricing, https://staging.example.com/contact. Check that heading structure, navigation landmarks, meta tags, and focus management are consistent. Report any page-specific issues and cross-page inconsistencies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-page consistency audit for a site section

output_examples.input: Audit https://staging.example.com for accessibility and SEO before deploy
output:
## Browser Audit Summary
- Target: https://staging.example.com
- Viewports: 1920px desktop, 375px mobile
- Automated checks: Lighthouse accessibility (92), SEO (100), best-practices (85)
- Manual checks: Keyboard navigation completed, contrast partially completed
- Verdict: Ready with fixes (1 P1, 3 P2 items)

## Findings
### Missing form labels on checkout fields
- Problem: Email and password inputs lack programmatically associated labels
- Severity: High
- Priority: P1 fix before deploy
- Evidence: input#email and input#password have placeholder attributes only; no label elements or aria-label
- WCAG: 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions
- Recommendation: Add label elements with for attributes matching input IDs

## Browser Audit Summary
- Target: https://staging.example.com/menu
- Viewports: 1920px desktop
- Automated checks: Lighthouse accessibility (78)
- Manual checks: Keyboard navigation completed
- Verdict: Blocked (1 P0 keyboard trap)

## Findings
### Keyboard trap in navigation dropdown
- Problem: Tab key cycles indefinitely within the main nav dropdown without Escape to close or a skip link
- Severity: Critical
- Priority: P0 deploy blocker
- Evidence: Tab through main nav items; focus enters dropdown but cannot leave without mouse click
- WCAG: 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap
- Recommendation: Close dropdown on Escape key; ensure focus returns to the parent menu trigger

best_practices: Always run automated Lighthouse checks first, then supplement with manual keyboard and focus testing for gaps automation cannot cover
Include specific WCAG success criterion numbers in findings so developers can reference the exact standard when fixing issues
Mark any manual checks you could not complete in the Residual Risk section so stakeholders understand the audit's blind spots
anti_patterns: Do not report issues found only in static source code unless you can confirm them in the rendered browser output
Do not claim WCAG compliance from this audit alone; use phrases like No blocking issues found or No automated failures detected
Do not produce vague findings like improve accessibility without specifying the affected element, user impact, and a concrete fix

faq.question: What does this skill check that Lighthouse alone cannot?
faq.answer: This skill adds manual browser checks for keyboard navigation, focus visibility, focus traps, hover-only content, form validation behavior, responsive layouts at 200 percent zoom, and composite widget keyboard patterns. These require human or AI visual inspection that automated tools cannot reliably verify.

faq.question: Can this skill certify WCAG 2.2 AA compliance?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides a pre-deploy audit to catch common issues. Formal WCAG conformance requires a complete accessibility audit by a qualified professional covering all pages, states, and user flows.

faq.question: Does the skill work with locally running development servers?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can audit localhost URLs if the AI has browser automation access. Provide the full local URL such as http://localhost:3000 and the skill will check the rendered page.

faq.question: What tools are needed to run this audit?
faq.answer: The skill uses Lighthouse CLI or the Chrome DevTools Lighthouse panel for automated checks, plus browser developer tools for manual inspection. No additional software is required beyond a Chromium-based browser.

faq.question: How long does a full audit take?
faq.answer: A single-page audit with automated and manual checks typically takes 10 to 20 minutes. Multi-page or complex application audits with authenticated flows will take longer.

faq.question: Can I audit pages that require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes, if you provide the authentication state, credentials, or session cookies. The skill can audit authenticated pages but mark any auth-gated flows that could not be tested in the Residual Risk section.
